headline: A China-linked spy crew rewired victims' own Google Workspace to siphon defense and medical research, while a one-click Microsoft 365 Copilot flaw and poisoned WordPress plugins widened the software attack surface.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Exposure
emerging_trends.text: The Copilot SearchLeak chain and research showing a 13-word Reddit snippet can hijack AI search both prove attackers keep turning trusted AI assistants into data-theft and disinformation channels.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply Chain
emerging_trends.text: Poisoned WordPress plugin scripts served from a tampered CDN show attackers favoring trusted update paths to reach thousands of sites at once.

emerging_trends.subject: State Espionage
emerging_trends.text: China's UNC6508 and North Korea's Contagious Interview both hunted researchers and developers, underscoring sustained nation-state pressure on knowledge workers.

emerging_trends.subject: Agent Identity
emerging_trends.text: Launches from Omada, 1Password, Delinea, Trust3, and NewCore all target the ungoverned sprawl of AI-agent and machine identities now reaching enterprise data.

emerging_trends.subject: Extortion Reach
emerging_trends.text: ShinyHunters claimed the Council of Europe and 137,000 Infinite Campus accounts in one day, showing how far its Salesforce-focused theft campaign now stretches.

topics.title: China's UNC6508 Loots Research Networks via REDCap
topics.latest_developments: Google's Threat Intelligence Group exposed UNC6508, a China-linked crew that hid for over a year inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks by compromising internet-exposed REDCap servers and rewiring victims' own Google Workspace filters to auto-copy outgoing mail.
topics.summary: The group breached vulnerable REDCap research platforms, deployed custom InfiniteRed malware to harvest login credentials, and stole sensitive defense and clinical research email. The exfiltration stood out: attackers repurposed the targets' Workspace forwarding rules to copy any message they wanted. Institutions running REDCap should patch, audit Google Workspace mail rules, and hunt for unauthorized forwarding filters.

sources.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
Chinese hackers breached North American research institutions via REDCap servers
Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America
Chinese hackers breach REDCap servers, steal medical research

topics.title: North Korea Weaponizes Developer Recruitment
topics.latest_developments: Proofpoint tied two fresh campaigns to North Korea's Contagious Interview cluster, also called Famous Chollima, which now lures software engineers with recruitment and code-review themes to turn development tools into malware delivery channels.
topics.summary: The actor poses as recruiters or reviewers and steers targets toward poisoned coding tasks that drop malware on their machines. The campaigns extend Pyongyang's long-running effort to compromise developers and cryptocurrency staff for theft and access. Engineers should treat unsolicited coding tests, recruiter repositories, and review requests as hostile until verified.

sources.title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels

topics.title: SearchLeak Turns Microsoft 365 Copilot Into a Data Thief
topics.latest_developments: Varonis Threat Labs chained three bugs into SearchLeak, a one-click attack that pulls emails, calendar entries, OneDrive and SharePoint files, and even MFA codes out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search through a crafted link on a genuine microsoft.com domain; Microsoft has patched the chain.
topics.summary: Because the malicious link pointed at a real Microsoft domain, URL filters and anti-phishing tools waved it through. SearchLeak belongs to a growing class of prompt-injection attacks that hide payloads in URLs and other variables, reaching enterprise data a victim never meant to share. Microsoft has fixed the flaw, and admins should confirm tenants run the patched build.

sources.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
New attack turned Microsoft 365 Copilot into 1-click data theft tool

topics.title: ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe and School Data
topics.latest_developments: ShinyHunters claimed it stole 297 GB from the Council of Europe, the continent's oldest intergovernmental body, which has opened an investigation, and separately surfaced as the gang behind a March Salesforce theft that exposed 137,000 Infinite Campus K-12 staff accounts.
topics.summary: The extortion group keeps mining Salesforce instances for sellable data. It threatens to leak Council of Europe records that allegedly include employee personal information, and the Infinite Campus haul hit staff at the widely used student information system. Organizations on Salesforce should tighten access, review connected apps, and warn affected staff to expect targeting.

sources.title: Council of Europe investigates ShinyHunters data breach claims
ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack
Infinite Campus data breach affects 137,000 school staff accounts

topics.title: Ransomware Stops the Mills at Mackay Sugar
topics.latest_developments: A ransomware crew calling itself The Gentlemen hit Mackay Sugar, Australia's second-largest sugar producer, and shut down its mills.
topics.summary: The attack halted milling at a major agricultural producer, idling operations that feed a regional supply chain. It marks another case of ransomware crossing from corporate IT into operational technology and food production. Mackay Sugar is working to restore service while the broader sector watches its exposure.

sources.title: Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia's Second-Largest Sugar Producer

topics.title: Tampered CDN Poisons Three Popular WordPress Plugins
topics.latest_developments: An attacker tampered with trusted JavaScript on Awesome Motive's content delivery network, poisoning the OptinMonster, TrustPulse, and PushEngage plugins so that an administrator loading a page would silently spawn an attacker-controlled admin account and a hidden backdoor plugin.
topics.summary: The malicious code fired only when a logged-in administrator loaded the file, leaving ordinary visitors untouched and the intrusion quiet. The three plugins run on large numbers of WordPress sites, giving the supply-chain attack broad reach through a single trusted CDN. Operators should rotate admin credentials, hunt for rogue admin accounts and unknown plugins, and confirm clean CDN assets.

sources.title: OptinMonster WordPress plugin hacked in CDN supply-chain attack
Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites

topics.title: UK Moves to Bar Under-16s From Social Media
topics.latest_developments: The UK's Department for Science, Innovation and Technology announced a ban on social media access for children under 16, covering all user-to-user platforms built for social interaction, public posting, and algorithmic feeds.
topics.summary: The rule targets platforms that let users post material and serve algorithmically ranked content to others. It follows age-gating moves abroad and sharpens pressure on companies to verify ages, a step that itself drives new data collection and privacy debate. Platforms operating in Britain will face fresh compliance and identity-verification demands.

sources.title: UK to ban social media access for children under 16

topics.title: Fake Filings Force Maine to Pull Its Breach Portal
topics.latest_developments: Maine took its public data-breach notification portal offline after someone filed fraudulent disclosures impersonating VRChat and Discord, and the attorney general will keep public access closed pending an audit of its procedures.
topics.summary: Companies can still report breaches to the state, but the public-facing portal stays dark while Maine reviews how fake submissions slipped through. The episode exposed weak validation in a transparency tool that journalists and the public rely on for breach data. The attorney general's office plans to restore access once it tightens its intake checks.

sources.title: Maine closes data breach portal to the public after fake reports
Maine Disables Data Breach Portal Due to Fake Submissions
Maine forced to take down data breach portal after fake notices filed with authorities

weather: Tuesday Night: Mostly Cloudy then Chance Rain Showers, low 58F.

games.recap: Meyer outduels Skenes, allows one run in six innings as Marlins top Pirates 4-2

business_politics.title: US-Iran Deal Reopens the Strait of Hormuz
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump said Monday the Strait of Hormuz will fully reopen by Friday and the deal text releases in the coming days, ships began moving through, and average US gasoline fell below $4 a gallon.
business_politics.summary: The United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding to end their war, dismantle Iran's nuclear program, and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the conduit for roughly a fifth of the world's seaborne oil. Oil settled at its lowest since March 4 and the Dow closed at a record. Shipping groups caution that traffic through the strait could take weeks to recover even half its prewar level.

sources.title: Stocks Have Even More Room to Fly if Hormuz Reopens
Trump Says Strait of Hormuz Will Be Fully Open by Friday Under Iran Deal
Average US petrol price falls below $4 on deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz

business.title: Pa. Court Rules Skill Games Are Slot Machines
business.latest_developments: The Pennsylvania Supreme Court ruled Monday that cash-payout skill games are slot machines and must follow the state's gambling law, an actual decision beyond last week's opinion-page debate over the devices.
business.summary: The ruling subjects the tens of thousands of skill game terminals in Pennsylvania bars, convenience stores, and clubs to the state's gambling regulations, settling a long-contested question with revenue stakes for operators and the state lottery alike.

sources.title: Skill games are slot machines, must adhere to Pa.'s gambling law, state Supreme Court rules

business.title: Pittsburgh International Named Among World's Most Beautiful Airports
business.latest_developments: The French organization Prix Versailles placed Pittsburgh International on its 2026 list of the world's seven most beautiful airports.
business.summary: The recognition honors the airport's new $1.7 billion terminal, opened last year, whose undulating roofline imitates the region's hills and rivers.

sources.title: Pittsburgh International named among world's most beautiful airports by prestigious organization

business.title: Cuban Food Truck Opens McKees Rocks Restaurant
business.latest_developments: Milanes Cuban Corner, a popular Pittsburgh food truck, has grown into a sit-down restaurant in McKees Rocks.
business.summary: Carlos and Collyn Milanes built a following selling pressed Cuban sandwiches, roast pork, and pickles from a truck and now run a brick-and-mortar restaurant.

sources.title: Pickles, pork and perfectly-pressed bread with Milanes Cuban Corner

around_town.title: Great Allegheny Passage to Close for Landslide Repairs
around_town.latest_developments: Crews will temporarily close a two-mile stretch of the Great Allegheny Passage this summer to guard against landslides.
around_town.summary: The trail links Pittsburgh to Cumberland, Maryland, and draws cyclists and hikers; the closure lets crews take preventative measures on a slide-prone section.

sources.title: Two-mile stretch of Great Allegheny Passage to close for landslide repairs

around_town.title: Pittsburgh Nears Fern Hollow Settlements
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh is approaching final settlements with most people hurt when the Fern Hollow Bridge collapsed.
around_town.summary: The Frick Park bridge fell in January 2022, dropping vehicles and a bus into the ravine; the city is now resolving the remaining claims years afterward.

sources.title: Years after the Fern Hollow Bridge collapse, Pittsburgh nears final settlements with most victims

around_town.title: City Pools Open for the Season
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh's public pools have opened for summer, and the city published hours and fees.
around_town.summary: The Post-Gazette laid out pool hours and entry fees along with other city summer events.

sources.title: Pittsburgh pools are open for the season. Here are the hours and fees, plus other summer events in the city

around_town.title: Transit Sets Juneteenth Schedule
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh Regional Transit announced its service levels for Juneteenth, the National Freedom Day holiday on June 19.
around_town.summary: The agency set its bus and rail schedule for the holiday so riders can plan around reduced service.

sources.title: Pittsburgh Regional Transit announces service schedule for Juneteenth National Freedom Day

around_town.title: Storm Cleanup Continues in Beaver and Butler Counties
around_town.latest_developments: The National Weather Service confirmed a tornado crossed into Beaver County, with no EF rating yet, as residents cleared downed trees and a Butler County farm assessed a machine shed torn from its foundation.
around_town.summary: Sunday's severe storms toppled trees onto homes and cars and cut power across Western Pennsylvania; survey teams are still rating the tornadoes.

sources.title: NWS confirms tornado crossed into Beaver County on Sunday
Machine shed ripped from foundation at Western Pa. farm after strong storms

events.title: App Maps Pittsburgh Film Locations
events.latest_developments: A self-guided tour app, PastFinders, guides users to Pittsburgh movie locations, among them the downtown spot where The Dark Knight Rises filmed.
events.summary: The app points to sites across the city used in films, letting residents and visitors stand where scenes were shot.

sources.title: You could be standing right where 'The Dark Knight Rises' was filmed in Downtown

around_teams.title: SNR Drive Ranks Divisions by Quarterback Talent
around_teams.latest_developments: On the Steelers' SNR Drive, Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler ranked every NFL division by its quarterback talent.
around_teams.summary: The team's show worked through all eight divisions, weighing where the AFC North and its passers stack up against the rest of the league.

sources.title: Ranking Divisions by QB Talent | SNR Drive | Pittsburgh Steelers

around_teams.title: Which Steelers Rookies Could Contribute Early
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette weighed whether rookies such as receiver Germie Bernard and offensive lineman Max Iheanachor look ready to contribute after OTAs and minicamp.
around_teams.summary: The piece assessed which members of the Steelers' draft class flashed enough in spring work to earn early roles.

sources.title: WATCH: Are any Steelers rookies looking like quick contributors after OTAs, minicamp?

around_teams.title: Termarr Johnson Trending Up in the Minors
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette's MiLB Monday examined whether former top Pirates pick Termarr Johnson is turning his season around in the minors.
around_teams.summary: The column tracked Johnson alongside other Pirates farmhands, gauging how the infielder's development is progressing.

sources.title: MiLB Monday: Is former top Pirates pick Termarr Johnson turning things around?

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: AI's Brokenomics
reading.summary: Zitron argues the economics of the AI industry are fundamentally unsound, with leading firms burning billions a year and no credible path to profit.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Responding to a survey of 6,000 digital workers, Newport contends that knowledge work was already dysfunctional before AI arrived, and the tools expose problems they did not create.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Anthropic's Safety Superpower
reading.summary: Ben Thompson argues that Anthropic's conviction in its own safety commitment gives the company license to aggressively favor its business and even challenge the U.S. government.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
